howItWorks.intro: This section 301 tariff calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Annual Cost
rules.formula: Cost = Import Value × Tariff Rate × Shipments
rules.explanation: Per-shipment tariff multiplied by annual frequency.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What are Section 301 tariffs?
faq.answer: Additional tariffs imposed on Chinese goods due to unfair trade practices, ranging from 7.5-25%.

faq.question: Can I get an exclusion?
faq.answer: Some products qualify for exclusions. Check USTR.gov for current exclusion lists.

faq.question: Is the Section 301 Tariff Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What import value should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate import value value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Section 301 Tariff Calculator?
sections.body: The Section 301 Tariff Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your import value, tariff list, annual shipments, you get instant results including tariff rate, tariff per shipment, annual tariff cost. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting tariff rate right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your import value and need to find the right tariff rate. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Import Value ($)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your import value in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Tariff List
howToSteps.description: Type or select your tariff list in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Annual Shipments
howToSteps.description: Type or select your annual shipments in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your tariff rate, tariff per shipment, annual tariff cost. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your import value before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both tariff rate and tariff per shipment — they work together to give you the full picture.
